summary: The team combined three complementary approaches: YOLO 2.5D models with two different backbones, a custom 3D CenterNet architecture with a 2D EfficientNetV2-S extractor, and stacked meta-classifiers (LightGBM, XGBoost, CatBoost). They treated 13 vessel locations as separate bounding box classes and adopted a 2.5D strategy by stacking adjacent slices into RGB channels without Z-spacing standardization. The final predictions were obtained by averaging outputs from the YOLO models, the 3D CenterNet model, and the three meta-classifiers, with a parallel ensemble structure proving superior to a series structure for the meta-classifiers.
extensive_summary:
## Overview & Core Architecture
The solution integrates three complementary model families to address both aneurysm presence classification and 13-class vessel localization. The first component consists of YOLO 2.5D models using two backbones: the standard `YOLOv11m` and a custom YOLO architecture with `timm/tf_efficientnetv2_s.in21k_ft_in1k`. Instead of standardizing Z-axis spacing, the team adopted a 2.5D strategy where adjacent slices are stacked into RGB channels (`R = slice i-1`, `G = slice i`, `B = slice i+1`). This approach improved cross-validation scores by over 0.02 despite the lack of Z-resampling.

## 3D CenterNet & Flayer Design
The second component, dubbed "Flayer," employs a slice-wise 2D encoder paired with a shallow 3D head. It uses a 2D ImageNet-pretrained EfficientNetV2-S in features-only mode, extracting penultimate feature maps. These are rearranged into 3D volumes and processed by a lightweight Conv3d-BN-ReLU head to produce 3D centerness heatmaps and sub-voxel offsets. An auxiliary 2D segmentation head supervises early-layer features using vessel masks derived from a 3D DynUNet trained on isotropic 0.7 mm resampled volumes.

## Meta-Classifier & Ensembling
The final stage uses a stacked ensemble of LightGBM, XGBoost, and CatBoost. The meta-classifiers ingest concatenated predictions from the YOLO models and the Flayer, along with patient metadata (age, sex). The team compared series and parallel input structures, finding that a parallel configuration combining all model outputs (44 features) yielded higher CV and private scores. Final probabilities are averaged across all base models and meta-classifiers.

## Training & Optimization Details
YOLO models were trained with mixup (0.4), mosaic (0.4), and custom fitness functions prioritizing mAP@50 and AUC. The Flayer model used AdamW with cosine annealing, AMP mixed precision, and a combination of CenterNet-style focal loss, L1 offset loss, BCE classification loss, and auxiliary Dice/BCE loss. Negative samples were sampled evenly (10 per series), while positive samples used all annotated slices.
competition_flow: Raw DICOM slices are processed into 2.5D triplets and 3D volumes, fed into YOLO 2.5D models and a 3D CenterNet-based Flayer for localization and classification, with outputs combined alongside patient metadata into parallel LightGBM, XGBoost, and CatBoost meta-classifiers to produce final averaged probabilities.
data_reading: DICOM slices sorted by spatial position (SliceLocation → ImagePositionPatient → InstanceNumber), converted to 2.5D RGB triplets for YOLO, and precomputed as `.npy` volumes for the Flayer model.
data_processing: Resized to 512×512 with min-max normalization; stacked adjacent slices into 2.5D RGB channels without Z-axis resampling. Negative samples sampled evenly (10 per series), positives used all annotated slices. YOLO training used mixup (0.4) and mosaic (0.4); Flayer used shared 2D affine warp augmentations and Albumentations normalization.
features_engineering: Patient metadata (age, sex) concatenated with model predictions for meta-classifiers; 13 vessel location classes treated as separate bounding box targets; 2.5D slice stacking for YOLO inputs.
loss_functions: custom fitness function (0.5 × mAP@50 + 0.25 × mAP@50-95 + 0.25 × mAUC)
cv_strategy: MultilabelStratifiedKFold stratified by aneurysm presence, all 13 vessel locations, and modality; K-fold protocol driven by training CSV split.
ensembling: Final probabilities are obtained by averaging outputs from the YOLO 2.5D models, the 3D CenterNet model, and the three meta-classifiers, with a parallel input structure for the meta-classifiers and Nelder-Mead optimization applied during CV.
insights: Using a 2.5D slice-stacking strategy without Z-axis resampling improved cross-validation scores by over 0.02.
Prioritizing mAP@50 over mAP@50-95 in the YOLO fitness function yielded better performance.
A parallel ensemble structure for meta-classifiers outperformed a series configuration.
Initial poor YOLO classification AUROC was traced back to an insufficient number of negative training samples.
critical_findings: Training ConvNeXt architectures in half precision caused the loss to quickly become NaN.
Modifying the YOLO neck and head based on P3 feature utilization did not improve validation scores.
Using 3D segmentation masks to filter invalid YOLO location predictions caused a significant drop in recall.
Applying wavelet and log-polar transforms to YOLO-extracted patches failed to improve performance.
what_did_not_work: Insufficient negative samples in early YOLO training caused poor classification AUROC.
Training ConvNeXt in half precision caused loss to quickly become NaN.
Modifying the YOLO neck and head yielded no validation improvement.
Using 3D segmentation masks to filter invalid YOLO location predictions caused recall to drop.
Wavelet and log-polar transforms on YOLO-extracted patches did not yield good results.
Keypoint-Based Dual-Graph Predictor was too time-consuming and led to timeouts.

other_solution_refs.description: BYU competition writeup referenced for YOLO+timm customization and fitness function insights
Public notebook with initial LB 0.69 score used as starting point
Inspiration for 2.5D models with auxiliary segmentation head
Reverse engineering notebook used to investigate YOLO feature utilization
Vessel segmentation code referenced for filtering invalid predictions
Failed attempt using wavelet and log-polar transforms on YOLO patches
BYU writeup referencing the Keypoint-Based Dual-Graph Predictor approach
